home.hero.title: PLAY PADEL IN KUTAISI
home.hero.subtitle: Experience the fastest growing sport in the world at our premier courts.
home.whoWeAre.description:
Love Padel Club is an idea born to bring the world’s fastest-growing sport to Kutaisi, a city rich in history but ready for something new.

We transformed a former Soviet factory into the first indoor padel club in Western Georgia.

The club features two fully panoramic premium padel courts, one pickleball court, changing rooms and bathrooms, a café, parking, and an outdoor space.
We aim to grow the padel community in Kutaisi and share our love for the game with everyone.
home.aboutPadel.description: Think tennis meets squash in a glass-walled cage — but way more fun. Born in Mexico in the 1960s, padel is now the fastest-growing sport in the world, with 25+ million players across 90 countries. And now it's coming to Kutaisi!
home.aboutPadel.feature1.title: Easy to Learn
home.aboutPadel.feature1.desc: Pick up a racket and play on day one. Seriously.
home.aboutPadel.feature2.title: Always Social
home.aboutPadel.feature2.desc: Played in doubles — you'll make friends every match.
home.aboutPadel.feature3.title: For Everyone
home.aboutPadel.feature3.desc: Kids, adults, beginners, pros — everyone belongs.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.general.a1: Padel is a fast-growing racket sport that combines elements of tennis and squash. It is played in doubles on an enclosed court surrounded by glass and mesh walls, which players can use during play.
faq.general.q2: Do I need experience to play?
faq.general.a2: Not at all! Padel is beginner-friendly and easy to learn. Many of our players are trying it for the first time.
faq.general.q3: How many people play on one court?
faq.general.a3: Padel is usually played as doubles, meaning 4 players per court (2 vs 2).
faq.general.q4: How long does a typical game last?
faq.general.a4: Most bookings are 60, 90 or 120 minutes, which is enough time for a full match.
faq.general.q5: Where is the padel club located in Kutaisi?
faq.general.a5: Our club is conveniently located on 7 Nikea Street with easy access from central Kutaisi by bus or car
faq.booking.q1: How do I book a court?
faq.booking.a1: Courts can be booked directly through our online booking system on the website
faq.booking.q2: How far in advance can I book?
faq.booking.a2: Courts can typically be booked up to 7 days in advance.
faq.booking.a3: Bookings can usually be cancelled or changed up to 24 hours before the start time. After that, the booking may be charged.
faq.equipment.q1: Do I need to bring my own racket?
faq.equipment.a1: No. Padel rackets are available for rent free of charge at the club.
faq.equipment.a2: Yes, we offer padel rackets, balls, grips, and accessories at our club shop.
faq.equipment.a3: Comfortable sportswear and non-marking court shoes are recommended.
faq.lessons.a1: Yes. We offer private lessons, group classes, and beginner sessions with certified padel coaches.
faq.lessons.q2: How do I book a lesson?
faq.lessons.a2: Lessons can be booked through the website or by contacting the club directly
faq.lessons.q3: Do you offer lessons for children?
faq.lessons.a3: Yes, we have junior programs and training sessions for kids and teenagers.
faq.facilities.a1: Yes, our club includes changing rooms and bathrooms.
faq.facilities.a2: Yes, we provide on-site parking for all guests
faq.facilities.q3: Do you have a café or lounge area?
faq.facilities.a3: Yes, players can relax in our club lounge/café area before or after matches.
faq.events.a1: Yes! We regularly host social tournaments, leagues, and club events for all skill levels.
faq.events.q2: Can I join games if I don’t have a partner?
faq.events.a2: Yes. Our player matching system helps connect players looking for partners or games.
faq.events.q3: Do you host corporate or private events?
faq.events.a3: Yes, we offer corporate events, team-building sessions, and private court bookings.
coaching.title: Coaching
coaching.intro:
Whether you're picking up a racket for the first time or looking to sharpen your competitive edge, our coaching programs are designed for every level.

Our experienced coaches offer a range of sessions including private lessons, group clinics, and structured courses tailored to help you improve quickly and confidently. We focus on technique, tactics, and game awareness, always in a supportive and enjoyable environment.
coaching.private.title: Private Coaching
coaching.private.desc: One-on-one sessions tailored entirely to your goals. Perfect for fast progress and personalized feedback.
coaching.group.title: Group Clinics
coaching.group.desc: Train alongside players of a similar level in dynamic, social sessions that combine coaching with match play.
coaching.beginner.title: Beginner Courses
coaching.beginner.desc: New to padel? Learn the fundamentals in a fun, welcoming setting and build a strong foundation from day one.
coaching.junior.title: Junior Coaching
coaching.junior.desc: Engaging sessions for younger players focused on skill development, confidence, and a love for the game.
coaching.outro:
No matter your starting point, our coaching is built to help you play better, feel more confident, and enjoy every moment on court.

Book your session through our coaches section on our booking system.
coaching.coaches.title: Coaches:
coaching.coach.parker.name: Parker Rhodes
coaching.coach.parker.subtitle: Meet Coach Parker
coaching.coach.parker.bio:
Originally from Newport Beach, California, Parker brings a truly international perspective to our coaching team. Having traveled extensively around the world to train and compete, he has developed a deep understanding of the game across different styles, levels, and competitive environments.

Parker has competed in multiple FIP tournaments globally and is currently ranked among the top 10 players in Georgia. His recent achievements include representing Team USA at the Pan American Championships in Paraguay—an experience that reflects both his high-level ability and commitment to the sport.

On court, Parker combines his competitive experience with a modern, approachable coaching style. Whether you're a beginner learning the fundamentals or an advanced player refining tactics, his sessions focus on smart positioning, technical precision, and real match play situations.
ballmachine.title: Ball Machine Training
ballmachine.subtitle: Padelmaster Ball Machine Training
ballmachine.intro:
Train smarter with our professional Padelmaster ball machine, the ultimate tool for accelerating your development.

The Padelmaster machine is specifically designed for padel, delivering precise and consistent shots that replicate real match situations. Whether you're working on your volleys, bandejas, smashes, or defensive shots off the glass, this machine allows you to practice with purpose.
ballmachine.features.title: Key Features:
ballmachine.features.1: Customizable shots – Adjust speed, spin, and trajectory
ballmachine.features.2: Programmable drills – Simulate realistic rally patterns
ballmachine.features.3: Consistent feeding – Perfect repetition to build muscle memory
ballmachine.features.4: Full-court coverage – Practice forehands, backhands, lobs, and net play
ballmachine.why.title: Why train with the Padelmaster?
ballmachine.why.3: Practice specific shots without relying on a partner
ballmachine.outro:
Whether you're a beginner building consistency or an advanced player fine-tuning your technique, the Padelmaster ball machine helps you get more quality practice in less time.

Book the Padelmaster ball machine under "Coaches" on our booking platform.
events.openPlay.title: OPEN PLAY
events.openPlay.heading: Open Play, Americanos & Socials
events.openPlay.intro: At our club, padel is more than just a sport—it’s a way to connect. Our Open Play, Americanos, and Social events are designed to bring players together in a fun, friendly, and inclusive environment.
events.openPlay.openPlay.title: Open Play
events.openPlay.openPlay.desc: Perfect for all levels, Open Play sessions let you jump into rotating matches throughout the evening. Come solo or with friends—our team organizes games so everyone gets a chance to play, meet new partners, and enjoy a relaxed, social atmosphere.
events.openPlay.americanos.title: Americanos
events.openPlay.americanos.desc: Looking for a bit of structure with a competitive twist? Americanos are fast-paced, organized events where players rotate partners and opponents each round, earning points individually. It’s a great way to challenge yourself, improve your game, and enjoy a lively, engaging format—no fixed teams, just great matches.
events.openPlay.socials.title: Socials
events.openPlay.socials.desc: Our Social events combine padel with a vibrant community vibe. Expect music, good energy, and a mix of casual games designed for fun and connection. Whether you’re here to play, meet new people, or simply enjoy the atmosphere, Socials are the perfect way to experience the club off the court as well.
events.openPlay.outro1: Join us and be part of a welcoming padel community where every session is as social as it is sporty.
events.openPlay.outro2: You can reserve your spot easily through the Events section of our online booking system.
events.openPlay.imageAlt: Padel open play session at our club
howToPlay.title: HOW TO PLAY
howToPlay.subtitle: Padel Rules
howToPlay.scoring.title: Scoring System
howToPlay.rule1: Padel uses the same scoring system as tennis. A match consists of three sets. Points are counted as 15, 30, 40, followed by deuce and advantage. In professional padel, at 40–40 a Golden Point is often used instead of advantage.
howToPlay.rule2: As in tennis, players switch sides on odd-numbered games (1–0, 2–1, 3–2, 4–3, 5–4, etc.).
howToPlay.rule3: Any player can hit the ball from anywhere on the court, except during the serve.
howToPlay.rule4: For a point to count, the ball must first bounce on the ground before hitting a wall. The only exception is the serve. After the bounce on a serve, the ball may only hit the glass wall not the fence. During rallies, the ball may hit both glass walls and fences. Players can hit the ball either before it bounces or after it bounces, depending on the situation.
howToPlay.serve.title: Serve
howToPlay.rule5: The serve is made from behind the service line. The first serve starts from the right side. The player has two attempts. As in tennis, the serve must be diagonal. Unlike tennis, the ball is not hit overhead. In padel, the player must strike the ball below waist level, around hip height.
howToPlay.rule6: If the first serve hits the net or is otherwise invalid, the player may serve again. If the ball hits the net and then does not land in the opponent's diagonal service box, the serve is considered a fault.
howToPlay.etiquette.title: Game Etiquette
howToPlay.rule7: A player is not allowed to start play until all players are in position and ready.
howToPlay.rule8: If you touch the net with your racket, clothing, or body during play, your opponent wins the point.
